EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Jump Input - Jump can be configured in the CharacterMovementComponent

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: out hit impact normal points away from the wall, can use that to make wall jump go away from wall

Blueprint.Name: BlueprintDescription
Blueprint.Value: The character you control in the game, includes firing logic

Blueprint.Name: BlueprintDescription
Blueprint.Value: GameMode that specified the pawn and HUD Blueprints to use

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Assign the Input Mapping Context for player controls.

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Add physics impulse to any physics object we hit

Blueprint.Name: BlueprintDescription
Blueprint.Value: Projectile fired by player that pushes physics objects

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: The SphereCollision component is set to only Overlap with the Pawn Object Type. Everything else will be ignored.

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Remove this pickup actor from the world

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Add the custom Skeletal Mesh Weapon component to our player character. It contains all the logic and input for the weapon.

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Does the character already have a weapon?

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value:
A cast is a hard reference that loads the referenced actor into memory when the actor that contains the cast is loaded. In this case, it's the best solution as we need a direct reference to the Pawn. Refer to the Actor Communication Documentation for more information on best Blueprint Communication practices:

https://docs.unrealengine.com/ProgrammingAndScripting/ActorCommunication/

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: For the purpose of the template, this is the only item that can be picked up, which is why this actor is a singleton. If this was designed to be a scalable framework for a full game, using inheritance (Actors that contain core logic and are inherited from), or composition (components that contain logic that can be shared across actors), is preferred.

K2Node_CommutativeAssociativeBinaryOpera.Name: NodeComment
K2Node_CommutativeAssociativeBinaryOpera.Value: Move spawn location forwards a bit

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: This input action has the "pressed" trigger so it only fires ones.

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Attach this skeletal component to the First Player Arm Mesh at the socket at the hand named GripPoint

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Notify the Anim Instance that we have a weapon

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: Play the montage that contains our FirstPersonFire Animation

EdGraphNode_Comment.Name: NodeComment
EdGraphNode_Comment.Value: With enhanced input we can add a new mapping context that configures the shoot action and enable the input. This could also be removed when the player drops the weapon. In this way you could even make updates to the base game introducing new input layouts without a monolitic long DefaultInput.ini

K2Node_CallFunction.Name: NodeComment
K2Node_CallFunction.Value: This is a generic way to get the camera rotation that doesn't require any hard references

K2Node_CallFunction.Name: NodeComment
K2Node_CallFunction.Value:
Sockets can be used for gameplay data that's different between
models without having to change your Blueprint logic
